title: Apple raises MacBook and iPad prices due to rising RAM costs
summary: The company increases the price of its computers and tablets by an average of 20%, while the iPhone is excluded from the increase.

title: Microsoft announces global price increase for Xbox Series X|S consoles
summary: The company increases the price of its consoles by up to $150, citing global economic conditions.

title: Rockstar reveals GTA VI pricing: digital version from €80 and complete edition for €100
summary: The anticipated game will include separate content for story mode and online, and will launch on November 19 exclusively in digital format.

title: US bans sale of Polestar electric vehicles in the country
summary: The Trump administration prevents the Swedish brand, partly owned by Volvo, from selling its cars in US territory, sparking criticism in the sector.

title: Polymarket suffers million-dollar theft and announces refunds to affected users
summary: The decentralized prediction platform was attacked by cybercriminals who stole user funds, and the company commits to returning the money.

title: White House asks OpenAI to delay launch of GPT-5.6 for security reasons
summary: The US government asks the AI company to halt the release of its new model while potential risks are assessed.

title: Micron secures record prices for DRAM and NAND memory with agreements through 2030
summary: The chip manufacturer signs long-term contracts that will keep memory costs high, impacting the consumer electronics market.

title: Notion shuts down its Notion Mail service to focus on AI agents
summary: The productivity platform decides to cancel its email app launched just over a year ago to redirect its efforts towards artificial intelligence.

title: Google launches dedicated Google Finance app for Android with real-time data and AI
summary: The new standalone app offers updated financial information and AI-powered features for investors.

title: Qualcomm enters the data center market with Dragonfly C1000 processor with over 250 cores
summary: The chip, based on Oryon architecture, reaches 5 GHz and is designed for AI workloads, competing with Intel and AMD.

title: Steam Machine: Valve removes reference to 4K/60 FPS, criticizes RAM manufacturers, and sells out in Japan
summary: News about Valve's console includes specification changes, complaints about memory prices, and high demand in Japan selling out stock.

title: IBM unveils the world's first sub-1 nanometer chip
summary: The American company achieves a milestone in semiconductor manufacturing with subnanometer transistors that promise to revolutionize performance and energy efficiency.

title: Herculaneum scroll read for the first time in 2,000 years thanks to AI
summary: A team of scientists uses artificial intelligence and X-rays to decipher the text of a scroll carbonized by the eruption of Vesuvius, revealing its content.

title: Google reveals Russian group Turla used STOCKSTAY backdoor in attacks on Ukraine
summary: Google's cybersecurity unit discovers a new backdoor used by Russian threat actor Turla in espionage operations against Ukraine.

title: FCC proposes canceling $2 billion program to connect schools to the Internet
summary: The FCC chairman argues screen time concerns, but critics accuse him of overstepping his role as a parent.

title: English hospitals declare critical incidents due to computer failures during heatwave
summary: High temperatures cause blackouts and failures in medical equipment and IT systems, forcing several hospitals to activate emergency protocols.

title: COVID vaccine study suppressed by CDC finally sees the light
summary: The research, which was blocked by the CDC director, is published and provides new evidence on the efficacy of the COVID vaccine.

title: World Cup teams turn to artificial intelligence to improve performance
summary: Teams around the world use AI tools for tactical analysis, injury prevention, and training optimization, in a parallel technological competition.

title: Rare book dealers fear tech companies are destroying unique editions to feed AI
summary: The digitization of historical works by AI companies is causing irreparable damage to unique copies, according to bibliophiles.

title: Anthropic's Claude gains market share among paying users against ChatGPT
summary: Anthropic's AI assistant is increasingly attracting paid subscribers, directly competing with OpenAI's dominance in the chatbot market.
